market_positioning.short_term_view_1_5d: Broad participation remains decisively weak, so the best 1-5 day setups are selective shorts and narrow momentum longs in commodities and energy rather than broad dip-buying.
market_positioning.swing_view: Swing opportunities are secondary and should stay selective, with commodity-linked leadership favored and oversold bond, utility, and international equity rebounds treated as tactical only.
supporting_signals: Breadth is extremely weak at 4 advancers versus 44 decliners.
Average 1-day return is deeply negative while volume and range ratios are elevated, signaling broad liquidation rather than isolated weakness.
Sector ETF 3-day continuation is concentrated in DBC, XLE, USO, PDBC, DBA, and grain ETFs, showing narrow commodity-led leadership.
Industry momentum is heavily concentrated in oil, gas, refining, coal, and related resource groups, confirming narrow leadership rather than broad strength.
Treasuries, aggregate bonds, investment grade, high yield, and EM bonds are all down over 1d and 3d, so fixed income is not offering a clean defensive confirmation.
TLT shows the largest treasury weakness with elevated volatility, reinforcing stressed cross-asset conditions.
conflicting_signals: Commodity and energy ETFs retain positive 3-day and 5-day momentum despite the weak tape.
Mean-reversion lists are crowded with bonds, utilities, and international ETFs, so a short-term oversold bounce is possible.
Some resource industries remain resilient on weekly and monthly measures even as the broader market sells off.

preferred_trade_types: paired long commodities vs short broad equities

evidence: SPY is down sharply over 1d, 3d, and 5d.
Breadth is 4/44 with negative average return and elevated participation.
International beta ETFs and cyclical proxies are also under pressure, supporting broad equity weakness.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Reduce if breadth improves materially and broad ETFs stop making lower 3-day lows.

evidence: USO has strong positive 1d, 3d, and 5d returns.
Industry momentum is dominated by Oil & Gas E&P, Integrated, Refining & Marketing, Equipment & Services, and Midstream.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Invalidate on loss of commodity leadership or if XLE/USO roll over while broad market weakness persists.

evidence: DBC and PDBC are positive over 3d and 5d.
DBA and CORN show positive short-term follow-through.
Industry leadership in coal and resource-linked groups supports the commodity complex.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Cut back if commodity ETFs lose positive 3-day momentum or leadership narrows to a single contract.

evidence: TLT, AGG, BND, LQD, HYG, JNK, TIP, and EMB are all negative over 1d and 3d.
TLT has the largest treasury drawdown with elevated vol and range ratios.
Credit weakness alongside treasury weakness signals unstable cross-asset conditions.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Hedge loses edge if bond ETFs stabilize together and credit starts outperforming duration on a 1-3 day basis.

evidence: Silver, Gold, and Other Precious Metals & Mining rank at the top of industry mean-reversion after steep weekly declines.
Downside is extended enough to support a tactical snapback if selling pressure pauses.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Only actionable on stabilization; avoid forcing longs if breadth remains deeply negative and metals continue closing weak.

allocation_buckets.theme: oversold defensive and international rebound watchlist
evidence: XLU, TLT, TIP, AGG, BND, EWJ, EWZ, and VEA appear on ETF mean-reversion lists.
These groups are stretched versus short-term averages and could bounce after liquidation pressure eases.
allocation_buckets.risk_or_invalidation: Not a primary allocation while breadth and bond volatility remain stressed; require clear downside deceleration before entry.
